title: actor_cache eviction is O(n) scan on every insert at capacity
description:
src/actor_cache.rs lines 106-113: when the cache reaches MAX_ENTRIES (10000), it finds the oldest entry via .iter().min_by_key() which scans all 10000 entries. This runs on every new actor fetch once the cache is full.

What goes wrong: at steady state with 10000 cached actors, every new actor lookup triggers a full HashMap scan under the Mutex lock, blocking all concurrent actor key lookups for the duration.

Fix: either (a) use an LRU cache (e.g., lru crate, or a linked list + HashMap), or (b) batch-evict: when full, remove all entries older than CACHE_TTL/2 in one pass, so eviction is amortized rather than per-insert.
